hadiths.ar:
2044  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ي َّاسِزٍ إَّلْح َّاذِمِ ق َّالَّ: ق ُّلْث ُّ لِلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ هَّلْ ي َّكُّون ُّ س َّهْزُّ رَّمَّص َّان َّ ي ِسْعَّه ً وَّ عِس ْ زِي ن َّ ي َّوْماً  
ق َّق َّال    
ق ال مصن ف  هذ إ إلكي اب   -  رص ى  إلله عن ه  -: من  ج الف  هذ ة إلأ ج ن ار وذ هث  إ لى إلأج ن ار إلموإق ق ه  للعامه  ق ى  ص ذها إي ق ى  كما 
ي ي ق ى إلعامه  ولأ ي كلم إ لأ ن الي ق ي ه  كان ن ا من  كان  إ لأ إ ن  ي كون  مسي زس ذإ ق ي زس ذ وي ن ي ن  له ق ا ن  إلن ذعه  إ ن ما ي ماب  وي ن طل ن ي زك   
ذ كزها ولأ ق وة  إ لأ ن الله.
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about the saying of Allah {SWT}, the Almighty:  
"And complete the number" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:185).  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Thirty days."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2043
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٤٣

hadiths.ar:
2045  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّعَّاوِي َّه َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ صِي َّامِ إ َّي  َّامِ إَّل ي  َّس ْزِي ق ِ ق َّال 
رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ عَّن ْ صِي َّامِهَّا ن ِمِن ًى ق َّا َّم َّا ن ِع َّي ْزِهَّا  ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْس
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Yasir Al-Khadim, who said:  
"I asked Al-Ridha {a.s}: 'Can the Month of Ramadan ever be 29 days?'  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'The Month of Ramadan is never less than 30 days.'"  
[AL SADUQ] 
The compiler of this book (may Allah {SWT} be pleased with him) comments: 
"Whoever opposes these narrations and adheres to those that align with the views of 
the general public in contradiction to them should be treated with caution, just as the 
general public is treated cautiously. Such individua ls should only be addressed with 
precautionary dissimulation (taqiyyah), whoever they may be, unless they are 
sincerely seeking guidance, in which case they should be guided and the truth clarified 
to them. For inn ovation is only eradicated and invalidated by refraining from 
mentioning it. There is no power except with Allah {SWT}."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Khasal, Part 2, p. 530 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 143 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 273 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 297 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2044
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٤٤

hadiths.ar:
2046  - وَّ ن َّهَّى رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ : عَّن ِ إَّلْوِصَّالِ ق ِى  إَّلصِّي َّامِ وَّ كَّان َّ ي ُّوَّإصِلُّ ق َّق ِي لَّ لَّه  ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّق َّالَّ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:   
"I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about fasting during the Days of Tashreeq.  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and 
his family) only prohibited fasting on those days in Mina. However, outside of Mina, 
there is no issue with fasting them.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 85 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 517 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2045
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٤٥

hadiths.ar: 2047  - وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم رَّة
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) 
prohibited continuous fasting (without breaking the fast at night). However, He {saws} 
himself practiced it. When He was asked about this, He {saws} said:  
"I am not like one of you. I remain in the presence of my Lord {AZJ}, and He provides me 
with food and drink."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 67 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 520 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2046
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٤٦

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"The continuous fasting (wisal) that is prohibited is when a person makes his evening 
meal (iftar) the same as his pre-dawn meal (suhoor)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 521 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2047
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٤٧

hadiths.en:
Zurara asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about fasting continuously throughout the 
year.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It has always been disliked."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2048
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٤٨

hadiths.ar:
2050   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْن َّز َّن ْطِى ِّ عَّن ْ هِس َّامِ ن ْن ِ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْ سَّعْذٍ إَّلْح َّق  َّاف ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق الَّ: كُّن  َّا عِن ْذَّةُّ 
ي َّمَّان ِي َّه َّ رِج َّالٍ ق َّذ َّكَّزْن َّا رَّمَّص َّان َّ ق َّق َّال   لأَّ ج َّاءَّ رَّمَّص َّان ُّ ق َّا ِن  َّ رَّمَّص َّان َّ إِسْم  
مِن ْ إ َّسْمَّاءِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ لأَّ ي َّح ِى ءُّ وَّ لأَّ ي َّذ ْهَّث ُّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ي َّح ِى ءُّ وَّ ي َّذ ْهَّث ُّ إَّلز  َّإن ِلُّ وَّ لَّكِن ْ ق ُّولُّوإ   «
مُّص َّاف   إ ِلَّى إَّلأِسْمِ وَّ إَّلأِسْمُّ إِسْمُّ إَّلل َّهِ  عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ هُّوَّ إَّلس  َّهْز 
عِي ذإً
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said:  
"There is no continuous fasting (wisal) in fasting, nor remaining silent for a day until 
night."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 520 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 
10, p. 522 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2049
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٤٩

hadiths.ar:
2051  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ع ِي َّاب ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّن ْ ج َّذِّةِ عَّلَّي ْهِمُّ إَّلس لاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلِى  ُّ  
ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  طَّالِث ٍ صَّلوَّإب ُّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ مَّص ٰان
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Al -Bazanti, from Hisham ibn Salim, from Sa’d Al -Khaffaf, 
from Abu Ja’far {a.s}, who said:  
"We were with Imam {a.s}, eight men, and the topic of Month of Ramadan  was 
mentioned.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'Do not say, "This is Ramadan," or "Ramadan has passed," or "Ramadan 
has come," for Ramadan is one of the names of Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic. 
It does not come or go; it is the transient (month) that comes and goes.  
Instead, say, "The Month of Ramadan," for the month is attributed to the name, and 
the name is one of the names of Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic. It is the month 
in which the Quran was revealed. Allah {SWT}, the Almighty, made it an example and a 
celebration.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 69 • Ma’ani al-Akhbar, Part 1, p. 315 • 
Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 376 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 319 • Tafsir al-Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 390 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol. 93, p. 376 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 166 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 244 • Mustadrak 
Al-Wasail, Vol. 7, p. 438 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2050
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥٠

hadiths.ar:
2052  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ صَّلَّوَّإب ُّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ ه ٍ مِن ْ س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ 
لِق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝ إ ُّجِل َّ لَّكُّمْ لَّي ْلَّه َّ إَّلصِّي ٰامِ إَّلز َّق َّث ُّ إ ِلىٰ ن ِسٰان ِكُّمْ ۝» »
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim, from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, from his father 
{a.s}, from his grandfather {a.s}, who said that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"Do not say 'Ramadan'; rather, say 'the Month of Ramadan,' for you do not know what 
'Ramadan' is."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 69 • The Virtues of the Three Months, 
Part 1, p. 93 • Ma’ani al-Akhbar, Part 1, p. 315 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 2, p. 81 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 376 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 319 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 320 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 377 • Bihar 
Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 377 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2051
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥١

hadiths.ar:
2053  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْق ُّص َّي ْلِ عَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق َّالَّ لِن َّعْص ِ مَّوَّإلِي هِ ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْق ِطْزِ وَّ هُّو  ي َّذْعُّو لَّه 
ي َّق َّن  َّلَّ إَّلل َّهُّ مِن ْك َّ وَّ مِن  َّا « 
ي َّا إِن ْن َّ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ ق ُّلْث َّ ق ِى  إَّلْق ِطْزِ س َّي ْن اً وَّ ي َّق ُّولُّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ َّص ْحَّى س َّي ْن اً ع َّي ْزَّةُّ ق َّق َّال ق ُّلْث ُّ لَّهُّ ق ِى  إَّلْق ِطْزِ ي َّق َّن  َّلَّ  
إَّلل َّهُّ مِن ْك َّ وَّ مِن  َّ ا لِأ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّعَّلَّ مِي ْلَّ ق ِعْلِى  وَّ إِسْي َّوَّي ْث ُّ إ َّن َّا وَّ هُّوَّ ق ِى  إَّلْق ِعْلِ وَّ ق ُّلْث ُّ لَّهُّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ َّص ْحَّى ي َّق َّن  َّلَّ إَّل ل َّهُّ مِن  َّا وَّ مِن ْك َّ  
لِأ َّن  َّا ي ُّمْكِن ُّن َّا إ َّن ْ ن ُّص َّحِّى َّ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّمْكِن ُّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّص َّحِّى َّ ق َّق َّذْ ق َّعَّلْن َّا ع َّي ْزَّ ق ِعْلِهِ
hadiths.en:
Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"It is recommended for a man to go to his wife on the first night of the Month of 
Ramadan, based on the saying of Allah {SWT}, the Almighty: 
'It has been made lawful for you to be intimate with your wives during the night of 
fasting.' (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:187)  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 180 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 
213 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 497 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 22, p. 730 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 349 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, 
Vol. 20, p. 129 • Tafsir al-Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 398 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 251 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2052
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥٢

hadiths.ar:
2054   -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّز َّإح  إَّلْمَّذَّإن ِن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال إ َّن ْ ي ُّصَّلِّى َّ وَّ لأَّ ي َّطْعَّمْ 
ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْأ َّص ْحَّى جَّي  َّى ي َّن ْصَّزِف َّ إَّلْأ ِمَّام
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Al-Fudhayl, from Al-Ridha {a.s}, who said:  
"Imam {a.s} said to one of his companions on the Day of Eid al-Fitr while supplicating for 
him:  
'O so-and-so, may Allah {SWT} accept (the deeds) from you and from us.'   
Then, on the Day of Eid al-Adha, Imam {a.s} said to him:  
'O so-and-so, may Allah {SWT} accept (the deeds) from us and from you.'   
I asked Imam {a.s}: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah {SWT}, on Eid al -Fitr you said 
something, and on Eid al-Adha you said something different. Why?'   
Imam {a.s} replied: 'Yes, I said to him on Eid al -Fitr, "May Allah {SWT} accept (the deeds) 
from you and from us," because he did the same as I did, and our actions were the 
same. But on Eid al-Adha, I said to him, "May Allah {SWT} accept (the deeds) from us and 
from you," because we are able to offer a sacrifice, whereas he is not able to do so. 
Thus, our actions differed.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 181 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1304 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 7, p. 477 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 49, p. 105 • A’lam al-Uloom, Vol. 22, p. 211 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2053
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥٣

hadiths.ar: 2055  - وَّ كَّان َّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ إ ِذ َّإ إ ُّي ِى َّ ن ِطِي ث ٍ ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْق ِطْزِ ن َّذَّإ َّ ن ِلِسَّان ِهِ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Jarrah al-Mada'ini, from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"Eat on the Day of Eid al -Fitr before you pray, but do not eat on the Day of Eid al -Adha 
until the Imam has completed the prayer and returned."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 168 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 138 
• Iqbal Al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 280 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1301 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Part 7, p. 444 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2054
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥٤

hadiths.ar:
2056 - وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلن  َّوْق َّلِى  ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إ ِن ِّى  إ َّق ْطَّزْب ُّ ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْق ِطْزِ  عَّلَّى طِي ن ِ إَّلْق َّن ْزِ وَّ ي َّمْزٍ  
ق َّق َّالَّ لَّه
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family), 
when presented with perfume on the day of Eid al-Fitr, would begin by applying it to his 
tongue.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2055
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥٥

hadiths.ar:
2057  -  وَّ ن َّط َّزَّ إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إ ِلَّى إَّلن  َّاسِ ق ِى  ي َّوْمِ ق ِطْزٍ ي َّلْعَّن ُّون َّ وَّ ي َّص ْحَّكُّون َّ ق َّق َّال   لِأ َّصْحَّان ِهِ وَّ 
إِلْي َّق َّث َّ إ ِلَّي ْهِمْ ن َّ ق ِي هِ ن ِطَّاعَّي ِهِ إ ِلَّى رِص ْوَّإن ِهِ ق َّسَّن َّق َّ ق ِي هِ 
ق َّوْم  ق َّق َّار ُّوإ وَّ ي َّح َّل َّف َّ إ ج َّزُّون َّ ق َّح َّان ُّوإ ق َّالْعَّح َّث ُّ كُّل ُّ إَّلْعَّح َّث ِ مِن َّ إَّلص  َّاجِك ِ إَّللا َّعِث ِ ق ِى  إَّلْي َّوْمِ إَّل َّ ذ ِى  ي ُّي َّاب ُّ ق ِي هِ 
إَّلْمُّحْسِن ُّون َّ وَّ ي َّح ِي ث ُّ ق ِي هِ إَّلْمُّق َّصِّزُّون َّ وَّ إَّي ْ مُّ إَّلل َّهِ لَّوْ كُّس ِف َّ إَّلْع ِطَّاءُّ لَّس ُّع ِلَّ مُّحْسِن   ن ِا ِجْسَّان ِهِ وَّ مُّسِى ء  ن ِا ِسَّاءَّي ِهِ
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Nawfali said to Imam Abu Al-Hasan {a.s}:  
"I broke my fast on the Day of Eid al-Fitr with the clay of the grave (Turbah) and dates."  
Imam {a.s} replied: "You have combined blessing and tradition (sunnah)."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2056
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥٦

hadiths.ar:
2058   -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّن َّان ُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّذِي زٍ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ ذِي ن َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ا مِن ْ عِي ذٍ لِلْمُّسْلِمِي ن َّ 
إ َّص ْحًى وَّ لأَّ ق ِطْزٍ إ ِلأ َّ وَّ هُّوَّ ي ُّح َّذ َّذُّ لأ ِلِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ ق ِي هِ جُّز ْن ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Imam Al-Hasan ibn Ali {a.s} observed the people on the Day of Eid al-Fitr playing 
and laughing. Imam {a.s} turned to his companions and said:   
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic, created the Month of Ramadan as a 
racecourse for His creation, where they compete in obedience to Him to attain His 
pleasure. Some have advanced in it and succeeded, while others have lagged behind 
and failed. Astonishing, truly astonishing, is the laughter and play of one on a day when 
the righteous are rewarded, and the negligent face disappointment. By Allah {SWT}, if the 
veil were lifted, the doer of good would be preoccupied with his goodness, and the 
sinner with his sins."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 181 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1338 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2057
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥٧

hadiths.ar:
2059  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ لَّطِي ف ٍ إَّلي  َّق ْلِي سِى  ُّ عَّن ْ رَّر ِي ن ٍ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم «
ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ن ِالس َّي ْف ِ وَّ سَّق َّظَّ ي ُّم َّ إُّن ْي ُّذِرَّ لِي ُّق ْطَّغَّ رَّإ ْسُّهُّ ن َّاذَّى مُّن َّاذٍ مِن ْ ن ُّطْن َّان ِ إَّلْعَّزْس ِ إ َّلأَّ إ َّي  َّي ُّهَّا إَّلْأ ُّم َّه ُّ إَّلْمُّي َّحَّي ِّزَّة ُّ 
إَّلص  َّال َّه ُّ ن َّعْذَّ ن َّن ِي ِّهَّا لأَّ وَّق  َّق َّكُّمُّ إَّلل َّهُّ لِأ َّص ْحًى وَّ لأَّ ق ِطْزٍ -  وَّ ق ِى  ج َّن َّزٍ إ ج َّز 
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم لِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Hanan ibn Sudair, from Abdullah ibn Dinar, from Abu Ja'far 
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, who said:  
"O Abdullah, there is no Eid for the Muslims, whether Eid al-Adha or Eid al-Fitr, except 
that it brings renewed sorrow to the family of Muhammad  {saws}."  
I asked: "Why is that?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Because they see their right in the hands of others."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 169 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 389 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 289 • Iqbal Al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 279 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1337 • Wasa’il Al-
Shi’a, Vol. 7, p. 475 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 88, p. 135 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2058
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥٨

hadiths.ar:
2060  - وَّرُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ج َّان ِزٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إلس َّلَّامُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: "إ ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ إ َّو َّلُّ ي َّوْمٍ مِن ْ  س َّو َّإلٍ ن َّاذَّى مُّن َّاذٍ: 
إ َّي  ُّهَّا إلْمُّو ْمِن ُّون َّ، إع ْذُّوإ إ ِلَّى ج َّوَّإن ِز ِكُّمْ، ي ُّم َّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إلس َّلَّامُّ:  ي َّا ج َّان ِزُّ ج َّوَّإن ِز ُّ إلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّج َّل َّ لَّي ْسَّث ْ كَّح َّوَّإن ِز ِ 
هَّو ُّلَّأءِ إلْمُّلُّوك ِ، ي ُّم َّ ق َّالَّ: هُّوَّ ي َّوْمُّ إلْح َّوَّإن ِز ِ".
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Latif Al-Taflisi, from Razin, who said that Abu 
Abdullah {a.s} said:  
"When Hussain ibn Ali {a.s} was struck with the sword and fell, and his head was about 
to be severed, a caller from within the depths of the Throne (of Allah {SWT}) called out:  
'O bewildered and misguided nation after your Prophet! May Allah {SWT} not grant you 
success for Eid al-Adha or Eid al-Fitr.'"  
In another narration: "for fasting or breaking fast."  
Then Abu Abdullah {a.s} said: "By Allah {SWT}, they were not granted success, nor will they 
be, until the avenger of Husayn ibn Ali {a.s} rises."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2059
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٥٩

hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far {a.s}, from his father {a.s}, who said:  
"When the first day of Shawwal arrives, a caller announces: 'O believers, hasten to 
your rewards!' Then Abu Ja'far {a.s} said: 'O Jabir, the rewards of Allah {SWT}, the Almighty 
and Majestic, are not like the rewards of these worldly kings.'  
Then Imam {a.s} added: 'It is the Day of Rewards.'”  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 168 • Iqbal Al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 282 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1310 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 371 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 7, p. 480

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 59, H. 2060
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٩، ح ٢٠٦٠

hadiths.ar:
2062  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّالِذٍ عَّن ْ سَّعْذِ ن ْن ِ سَّعْذٍ إَّلْأ َّس ْعَّزِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ 
إَّلْق ِطْزَّة ِ كَّمْ ي ُّذْق َّغُّ عَّن ْ كُّلِّ رَّإ ْسٍ مِن َّ إَّلْحِن ْطَّه ِ وَّ إَّلس  َّعِي زِ وَّ إَّلي  َّمْزِ وَّ إَّلز  َّن ِي ث ِ  ق َّال 
إ لِهِ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Ibn Abi Najran and Ali ibn Al -Hakam, from Safwan Al -
Jammal, who said: "I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about Zakat al-Fitr.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'It is obligatory for the young and the old, the free and the enslaved. For 
every individual, it is one sa' (measure) of wheat, or one sa' of dates, or one sa' of 
raisins.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 171 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 71 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 80 • Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 46 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 234 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 
9, p. 327 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 60, H. 2061
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٠، ح ٢٠٦١

hadiths.ar:
2063  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّجْمَّذَّ ن ْن ِ ي َّحْي َّى عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْهَّمَّذ َّإن ِى ِّ وَّ كَّان َّ مَّعَّن ا جَّاج  اً ق َّالَّ: كَّي َّن ْث ُّ  
إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّلَّى ي َّذِ إ َّن ِى  ج ُّعِلْث ُّ ق ِذَّإك َّ إ َّن  َّ إ َّصْحَّان َّن َّا إِج ْي َّ لَّق ُّوإ ق ِى  إَّلص َّاعِ ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ ي َّق ُّولُّ إَّلْق ِطْزَّة ُّ 
ن ِصَّاعِ إَّلْمَّذَّن ِى ِّ وَّ ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ ي َّق ُّولُّ ن ِصَّاعِ إَّلْعِزَّإق ِى ِّ ق َّكَّي َّث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِلَّى رْطَّالٍ ن ِالْمَّذَّن ِى ِّ وَّ ي ِسْعَّه ُّ إ َّرْطَّالٍ  
ن ِالْعِزَّإق ِى زَّن ِى
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Khalid, from Sa’d ibn Sa’d Al-Ash’ari, who 
said:  
"I asked Abu Al-Hasan Al-Ridha {a.s} about Zakat al-Fitr: how much should be given per 
head from wheat, barley, dates, and raisins?"   
Imam {a.s} replied: "One sa' (measure) according to the sa' of the Prophet (peace and 
blessings be upon him and his family)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 171 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 80 • 
Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 46 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 251 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 332 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 60, H. 2062
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٠، ح ٢٠٦٢
